insights.context: Analysis of Cauterizer's optimal purchase timing and role in army composition.

insights.insight: Gaussite Symbiote's click is mathematically weak (~10-11 efficiency) because it requires floating green for multiple turns. The unit is more valuable for the Conduit component than the attacker component, and the click should only be used if it forces opponent into inefficient soak purchases.
insights.author: xnor_gate, mtanzer, anesotericman
insights.context: Analysis of why Symbiote's click efficiency is lower than expected despite Symbiote being a reasonable unit purchase.

insights.insight: Splitter is more vulnerable to Apollo snipes than Tarsier because Tarsier comes in pairs naturally and has an extra turn of protection; Splitter is the worst snipeable attacker to buy one-at-a-time against Apollo.
insights.author: masn6811
insights.context: Comparison of snipeable attacker vulnerability to Apollo.

insights.insight: Splitter is a useful unit in Redeemer mirrors because 1 damage is a better damage number to hit than 3; it blocks effectively on incoming Redeemer charge turns and can attack on other turns, enabling flexible openers like DDB into DCS into WCD.
insights.author: mqp
insights.context: Analysis of Splitter's role in Redeemer set openers.

insights.insight: Iceblade loses almost always against green Blood Pact strategies. Even when a set appears to support Iceblade, green-based units like Asteri beat it, making Iceblade BP rarely viable.
insights.author: masn6811
insights.context: Discussion of Iceblade matchups in sets with strong green alternatives.

insights.insight: Iceblade is weak against Energy Matrix+Walls combination because Iceblade has odd health, making it difficult to efficiently damage through the absorber-wall defense.
insights.author: darkeisbein
insights.context: Unit matchup analysis in defensive scenarios.

insights.insight: Odd-numbered health defenders are strong against Iceblade because Iceblade freezes units without killing them. Walls (2 HP, fragile) are essential defense against Iceblade due to their odd breakpoint.
insights.author: silentslayers
insights.context: Explanation of defender selection against Iceblade.

insights.insight: Animus's lack of granularity can force red waste, but it remains a good deal if the game is fast enough that building additional drones to spend wasted red is impractical; efficient attackers (like Shadowfang) can be built despite one red waste.
insights.author: velizar_
insights.context: Discussion of Animus efficiency and resource waste in fast games.

insights.insight: Buying a second Animus is generally only justified if there exists a unit costing 3+ red or a unit with red-costed click ability, except in rare very-high-econ situations.
insights.author: awaclus
insights.context: Analysis of multiple Animus purchases and their justification.

insights.insight: Mobile Animus is not efficient for economy compared to natural Animus or Drone economy; it is primarily justified when red granularity is critical to executing a strategy.
insights.author: awaclus, silentslayers
insights.context: Evaluation of Mobile Animus economy efficiency.

insights.insight: Natural Animus is slow compared to DD/DDE; it may enable turn 4 Rhino and Phage build but requires red waste if a second Rhino or Phage is desired, making DD/DDE preferable in many cases.
insights.author: awaclus
insights.context: Comparison of Natural Animus vs DD/DDE economy.

insights.insight: Evil Animus is a strong punish against double Thorium Dynamo, especially with support units like Electrovore; it forces the Dynamo player into Breachproof or fast attack.
insights.author: steel0229e, darkeisbein
insights.context: Discussion of effective responses to greedy Dynamo strategies.

insights.insight: Evil Animus (buying Animus after your first Dynamo instead of 2 Drones to enable fast red attackers like Grimbotch or Vore) can be superior to standard Dynamo openings in aggressive sets with low absorb, because it lets you build 2 red units next turn and pressure the opponent.
insights.author: liadahlia, masn6811
insights.context: Discussion of an unconventional Dynamo-era opening pattern.

insights.insight: Evil Animus (early Animus in Thorium sets) performs exceptionally well with Grimbotch or Feral Wither first because these cheap attackers exploit low-defense opponents and can transition into permanent attack more efficiently than the defending player can scale their defense.
insights.author: leary93, swapgo
insights.context: Named strategy pattern for early Animus aggression in specific sets.

insights.insight: Crazimus (Animus) is strengthened by units like Smorchling and Perforator, enabling aggressive early builds that threaten quick Rhino or Vore access.
insights.author: silentslayers
insights.context: Discussion of Crazimus synergies in vore/smorc sets.

insights.insight: Gauss Cannon is good in breachproof but generally shouldn't be a main attack force; it's worthwhile when it will deal damage and player would otherwise float excess gold/green, but Xaetron or other units typically provide better value.
insights.author: zera777
insights.context: Evaluation of Gauss Cannon value in specific game situations.

insights.insight: Hannibul is valuable in sets with Bloodragers because it blocks threat while still dealing damage; its 7 damage soak helps handle large Bloodrager-based attacks in late game.
insights.author: awaclus
insights.context: Unit synergy analysis in specific game with Bloodrager threats.

insights.insight: Flameanimus with wasted red is inefficient; Conduit or Blastforge for fast Bombardiers is often better than second Flameanimus when unable to spend all generated red.
insights.author: pikachumemes
insights.context: Evaluation of second Flameanimus vs alternative tech in specific replay.

insights.insight: Doomed Drone is fundamentally a supply-smoothing tool, not primarily valued for its blocking capability; the 1-gold cost difference between Doomed and regular Drone represents the value of having a unit available 5 turns later, and Doomed Drone is worse at long-term economy growth (1.228 growth factor vs Drone's 1.28).
insights.author: awaclus, silentslayers, consensus
insights.context: Extended discussion on Doomed Drone economics, opportunity cost, and comparison to regular Drones and hypothetical bt5 Drones.

insights.insight: A hypothetical 1-gold buildtime-5 Drone (no energy cost) would have a growth factor of 1.3247 towards infinity, making it superior to Wild Drone (1.28) but inferior to Tarsier (1.39-1.40 assuming Breach Vanguard costs 2.33-2.40 gold).
insights.author: silentslayers, awaclus
insights.context: Mathematical analysis of hypothetical unit economics using Wolfram Alpha growth rate calculations.

insights.insight: Replacing Drones with Forcefield provides roughly 2.66 Block Value per 4 Gold invested (2 prompt defense), compared to Wall's ~2.22 BV. This makes Drone-to-Forcefield replacement worth considering only if you have Green that would otherwise be wasted.
insights.author: liadahlia
insights.context: Calculation of the block value efficiency of replacing Drones with Forcefield.

insights.insight: The first Xenonbus is overpowered as an absorber, but all subsequent Xenonbus and absorbers in general are weak; Xenonbus is inefficient as an attacker or blocker.
insights.author: mrguy888
insights.context: Balance evaluation of Xenonbus unit.

insights.insight: In sets with both Cryo Ray and Iceblade, buying a single Iceblade with 3+ Cryos to freeze matrix is more efficient than buying multiple Iceblades; however, opponent's Grenade Mech + Fab setup can prevent Iceblade from staying safe.
insights.author: steel0229e, 307th
insights.context: Set reading discussion about tech decisions and unit combinations.

insights.insight: Proto (Protoplasm) is a better absorber than Wall on some defensive turns, making it preferable to buy in certain sets. In sets with good absorbers like Protoplasm, buying soak Rhinos is often a mistake compared to buying the unit with better absorb.
insights.author: mrguy888
insights.context: Reviewing a player's decision to buy soak Rhinos instead of utilizing better absorbers.

insights.insight: Protoplasm is not good unless it is actively absorbing or threatening to absorb. In sets without absorb supporters, Protoplasm loses most of its value.
insights.author: masn6811
insights.context: Explanation of Protoplasm's dependence on absorb mechanics.

insights.insight: Protoplasm is a superior absorber compared to Wall, which affects opening decisions when Protoplasm is in the set alongside early attack threats.
insights.author: masn6811
insights.context: Analysis of why turn 1 Animus is problematic in sets with Electrovore and early Smorcus+E damage.

insights.insight: The trade-off for buying Protoplasm (7GGR + 1 extra defense this turn) to get 4 attack and 4 defense next turn becomes less attractive when good soak is available, lowering the payoff value.
insights.author: jamberine
insights.context: Economic analysis of Protoplasm's value curve based on soak quality.

insights.insight: Fully synced Scorchilla attacks (holding Scorchillas to attack simultaneously every other turn) are less efficient than semi-synced (mixing Scorchilla and Tarsier). While synced Scorchilla denies absorb for 2/3 of turns, the value lost from delaying multiple Scorchillas outweighs this benefit around the 3-5 Scorchilla range.
insights.author: .bky_1556
insights.context: Debating optimal Scorchilla synchronization strategy in base+Scorchilla.

insights.insight: Scorchilla's main value is against Colossus due to exceptional hold value, but is otherwise outclassed by other attackers in most sets.
insights.author: xyotsuba, amalloy
insights.context: Niche application evaluation for specialized unit.

insights.insight: Hound (presumably a unit) is better than Mahar Rectifier as an attacker, but sometimes the engineer attached to it doesn't generate value. However, Mahar Rectifier's 2/5 body makes it very efficient for defending, offsetting Hound's superior attack role.
insights.author: masn6811
insights.context: Comparing two medium-sized attackers and their flexibility.

insights.insight: Hannibull (Hannibull) is relevant in low-economy games where the goal is to keep the opponent from ever reaching 7 attack to activate it. Two damage for 7BR is significant value, making Hannibull rush strategies legitimate in such contexts.
insights.author: consensus
insights.context: Discussing Hannibull's clickability and rush viability.

insights.insight: Flame Animus in a set where red units are weak (e.g., dominated by green/blue attackers) should be deprioritized in favor of pressure units like Fission Turret and Doomed Mech with Steelforge support.
insights.author: steel0229e
insights.context: Set analysis where red tech is not optimal.

insights.insight: Flame Animus synergizes with Smorcus/Perfector because both want red production and benefit from buying blue attackers at the same time, making the combination significantly more powerful than simply comparing individual unit efficiency numbers.
insights.author: jamberine
insights.context: Discussion of unit synergies and why raw cost-efficiency comparisons miss interaction value.

insights.insight: Steelforge enables blue breach-proof in limited cases (e.g., Odin synergy), but converting drones to damage via Steelforge requires red investment, which contradicts the breach-proof goal; therefore, pure mono-blue breach-proof with Steelforge is not viable.
insights.author: steel0229e
insights.context: Discussion of breach-proof unit synergies and constraints.

insights.insight: Steelforge is a convenient blue sink that enables drone-selling for breach threat and provides weak defense (2 health for 4BB), making it a transition unit rather than a core defense strategy.
insights.author: darkeisbein, liadahlia
insights.context: Analysis of Steelforge's role in defense and breach transitions.

insights.insight: When facing Iceblade Golem, splitting Infusion Grids (buying multiple copies) is beneficial defensively only if the player also has an Animus to generate extra defense; without Animus support, splitting Infusions is not worth the cost commitment.
insights.author: Arkanishu
insights.context: Discussion of anti-Iceblade Golem defensive strategies.

insights.insight: Fission Turret vs Geese (Bloodragers): When spamming Fission Turrets (which are not breach-capable), 2 extra Drones from Player 2 represent less value loss than when spamming breach units, since both are temporary army-builders with limited utility later.
insights.author: spiritfryer
insights.context: Discussion of unit type impact on the value of the 2-Drone compensation for Player 2 initiative delay.

insights.insight: Fission Turret has approximately 115% inflation relative to Drone and 105% relative to Tarsier without click value; including its click makes it highly efficient.
insights.author: namington
insights.context: Comparative inflation analysis of lifespan attackers.

insights.insight: Fission Turret clicks should be used to provide energy for defense (like Engineer purchases) in defensive scenarios, but should not be artificially forced if adequate green sinks are unavailable.
insights.author: consensus (miuna6933, namington)
insights.context: Discussion of Fission Turret resource sink decisions.

insights.insight: Auride Core is worth evaluating carefully: it only provides value if clicked frequently from the turn it's created; if left idle the first two turns after purchase, a Drone + Conduit is likely superior despite Core's potential.
insights.author: itsotter, spiritfryer
insights.context: Game analysis of Auride Core efficiency and timing.

insights.insight: Frostbrooder is countered hard by Xeno Guardian. If opponent builds Xenos to counter Brooders, you at least inconvenience their economy, but Tarsiers are generally preferable to Brooders if available.
insights.author: amalloy, Deleted User
insights.context: Matchup analysis of Frostbrooder vs Xeno Guardian in Centurion sets.

insights.insight: Valkyrie without combos (Cryo Ray, absorb setups) is roughly Borehole Patroller tier in threat value; it becomes significantly stronger when paired with synergistic units or favorable defense configurations.
insights.author: apooche
insights.context: Evaluating Valkyrie's baseline power level without synergies in a mixed-unit set.

insights.insight: Immolite increases Valkyrie's viability significantly; the line DD/DDC/DDB/DA+Oxide/Valk+Immolite sets up threatening builds and is cleaner than delayed Thunderhead pivots.
insights.author: leary93
insights.context: Discussion of synergistic unit combinations for specific set types.

insights.insight: Sentry soft-counters both Frostbite and Hellhound; it is a high-value unit in sets featuring Feral Warden + Frostbite strategies, making it the recommended defensive approach.
insights.author: liadahlia
insights.context: Brief strategic note on Sentry as a defensive tool in Feral Warden sets.

insights.insight: Endotherm Kit is rarely correct (≈90% of the time) if Ossified Drone is available in the set, due to Ossified Drone's vigilance preventing frostbite damage without requiring unclicked drones.
insights.author: namington
insights.context: Comparison of Endotherm Kit and Ossified Drone as anti-pressure defensive tools.

insights.insight: Endotherm Kit and Wincer together are overcommittal against Venge Cannon strategies because both units reduce defensive flexibility; purchasing raw attack (like Tarsier) is typically better than splitting investment between defensive utility units.
insights.author: xyotsuba
insights.context: Game-specific analysis of why defensive utility units fail against Venge Cannon.

insights.insight: Endotherm Kit functions similarly to Area of Effect (AoE) in Prismata because it affects multiple units (all of one type) at a single cost, making it difficult to balance without being either overpowered or situational.
insights.author: cactus_wren
insights.context: Comparison of Endotherm Kit's AoE-like mechanic to true area effects.

insights.insight: Endotherm Kit is not viable in Base+1 and performs even worse against breachproof attackers. Iso Kronus becomes stronger when facing bigger absorbers and matters more in absorb-denial scenarios.
insights.author: lyra1712
insights.context: Analysis of unit viability in limited sets.

insights.insight: Polywall is not particularly effective against early Tarsier spam because it provides 2 absorb on turn 1, 4 absorb on turn 2, then 0 absorb on turn 3, making it unreliable early. It is better used for soak value later in the game.
insights.author: amalloy, leary93
insights.context: Discussion of Polywall's limitations against early Tarsier pressure.

insights.insight: Ice Blade Golems counter blockers with even health like Infusion Grid, and are especially good against Doomed Wall.
insights.author: swapgo
insights.context: Ice Blade Golem matchup discussion.

insights.insight: Ice Blade Golems are good counters to Plexo Cell.
insights.author: liadahlia
insights.context: Matchup discussion between Ice Blade Golem and Plexo Cell.

insights.insight: Boreholes are more efficient than Tarsier in certain sets, making them preferable attackers when efficiency permits.
insights.author: namington
insights.context: Set-dependent attacker efficiency comparison.

insights.insight: Corpus click (sacrifice) provides efficient defensive soak when combined with high-threat units like Vai Mauronax and Brooder, as it allows defending against freeze threats without losing the Corpus unit.
insights.author: extratricky
insights.context: Discussion of Corpus synergy in defensive situations.

insights.insight: Grenade Mech can be understood as converting 6 Drones into 3 Tarsiers in disguise, by spending Blastforge resources on Pixies that scale equivalently to attack units.
insights.author: amalloy
insights.context: Conceptual framing of Grenade Mech's value.

insights.insight: Egg Turbine has a superior durability-to-gold-income ratio (2.0) compared to other drones like Thorium Dynamo (1.6), Trinity Drone (1.67), Wild Drone (1.5), and Vivid Drone (0.33), making Base + Egg Turbine + Fabrication potentially stronger breach-proof than Base + Trinity + Fabrication.
insights.author: shadourow
insights.context: Analysis of drone cost-efficiency ratios for breach-proof strategies.

insights.insight: Against an opponent building many Hannibulls, Chronofilter is a critical tech purchase to manipulate Hannibull attack timings; skipping it significantly reduces defensive flexibility.
insights.author: cactus_wren
insights.context: Specific example of Chronofilter's importance in Hannibull-heavy games.

insights.insight: Immolite is approximately the same efficiency as Tarsier, but performs worse as a first attacker since it cannot efficiently ramp attack against the first defensive unit.
insights.author: leary93, Deleted User
insights.context: Efficiency comparison of red attackers in early game.

insights.insight: Brooder is a strong counter to Plexo Cell because it forces Plexo Drones into short lifespan, making multiple Plexo Drones simultaneously create cascading inefficiency.
insights.author: mrguy888, xyotsuba
insights.context: Analysis of unit interactions in freeze-heavy sets.

insights.insight: Plexo Cell is effective soak against Plasmafier, but only moderately. Plexo can repeatedly block Plasma attacks, but the Plasma player can choose to hold Plasmafier units to avoid wasting them. Early Plasmafier into Plexo creates a tempo swing where the Plasma player spends 12GGGB while Plexo player spends 2GG, initially favoring Plexo.
insights.author: cogito3, swapgo, cachotognax
insights.context: Analysis of Plasmafier vs Plexo Cell interaction and holding strategy.

insights.insight: Plasmafier has positive self-synergy: high efficiency drives up inflation, which lowers drone value, making Plasmafier even more efficient relative to buying drones.
insights.author: masn6811
insights.context: Economic analysis of Plasmafier's meta-effect.

insights.insight: Megadrone absorption is slightly better than absorbing on Rhino, but absorbing on Rhino is considered poor play. Holding Megadrone is preferable to soaking with it defensively.
insights.author: xujhan, xyotsuba
insights.context: Brief comparison of absorption unit efficiency.

insights.insight: Mega Drone enables building attack early in red-heavy sets; absorbing on Mega Drone wastes 4 gold per turn but denies opponent's absorption-denial strategy.
insights.author: torikunsnaps
insights.context: Discussion of Mega Drone's role in red rush prevention.

insights.insight: Holding Mega Drone for defense (instead of buying a Wall) is mathematically favorable by default in Wall absorb sets, but practically overdefends that turn and wastes Blastforge resources that would better be spent on Walls.
insights.author: xnor_gate, jamberine, anesotericman
insights.context: Explanation of why mathematical efficiency and practical decision-making diverge for Mega Drone holds.

insights.insight: Botch Pillar should almost always be bought instead of Tarsier when available, as it provides superior value; in a set with good Botch availability, first priority should be multiple Botches before considering Tarsiers.
insights.author: medar
insights.context: Unit choice priority between common attackers in mixed sets.

insights.insight: Bloodrager is highly inefficient against Apple (likely referring to a defensive apple-like unit) and should not be built in sets where it cannot be utilized effectively.
insights.author: amalloy
insights.context: Replay analysis of poor Bloodrager purchases

insights.insight: Bloodrager can often be bought for only one real damage cost if timed correctly to deny some opponent absorb, making it a high-value early purchase in the right context.
insights.author: .bky_1556
insights.context: Explanation of Bloodrager efficiency in a specific game position.

insights.insight: Bloodrager typically ties with two Tarsiers when denying 1 absorb, but is superior when denying 2 absorb; without absorb denial Bloodrager has lower early threat but can be used in rare high-economy situations where you don't want to invest in more tech.
insights.author: spiritfryer, shadourow, cooledcannon
insights.context: Analysis of Bloodrager efficiency compared to Tarsiers in different absorb denial scenarios.

insights.insight: Centrifuge (Fuge) into Omega using blue economy is viable if you include red generation (Smorcus) to spend excess red, avoiding pure blue sink into Omega which is too slow.
insights.author: amalloy, shadourow
insights.context: Opening theory discussion for Fuge-based Omega acceleration

insights.insight: Fuge into Wincer has a vulnerability: P2 fast Animus into Fuge can punish P1 T1 Fuge due to economy timing differences.
insights.author: masn6811, darkeisbein
insights.context: Meta discussion on Fuge opening matchups

insights.insight: Defense Grid is often better than Lucina in Blue mirrors because Omega Splitter and Grenade Mech make it hard to justify both; if forced to choose, Defense Grid is higher priority.
insights.author: masn6811
insights.context: Analysis of unit priority in Blue mirror sets.

insights.insight: Against Energy Matrix (a prominent soak unit), fast efficient attackers like Hellhound become significantly less valuable because the absorb barrier is difficult to break through quickly, shifting the game toward slower attack that can accumulate past the barrier.
insights.author: deadhour
insights.context: Analysis of Hellhound overvaluation in a set with high-health absorbers.

insights.insight: Mahir Rectifier is especially strong in Scorch sets because it can be clicked to eliminate rotting absorber, providing clean defense against 0 attack; easy to obtain since it costs green like Scorchillas.
insights.author: mrguy888
insights.context: Analysis of missed unit synergy in Scorch game replay.

insights.insight: Arms Race Titan is an effective counter to Antima Comet if built after P1's Antima, because the accumulated soak from Arms Race becomes increasingly valuable over time while the opponent's soak from defending early remains static.
insights.author: velizar_
insights.context: Discussion of dynamic defensive units as Antima counters.

insights.insight: Mahar is commonly bought in sets where it is available due to efficient cost, but buying multiple Mahars for absorb denial alongside other red attackers can be suboptimal. The first Mahar effectively acts like a 7GG Energy Matrix (forcing over-defense by 2), while subsequent Mahars are just solid attackers.
insights.author: zera777
insights.context: Discussion of Mahar value and over-commitment to absorb denial.

insights.insight: In sets with natural Conduit, player 2 has an advantage in obtaining green tech for units like Tia Threnody, because Conduit produces green at the start of P2's turn naturally.
insights.author: jamberine
insights.context: Analysis of turn order advantage with natural blue tech producers.

insights.insight: In sets with Infusion and green units, buying multiple Conduits early can be inefficient without a strong green sink like Cluster Bolt to justify the density.
insights.author: shadourow
insights.context: Discussion of Conduit density and green sink requirements.

insights.insight: Forcefield is more gold-efficient for soak than Rhino, but if you buy Forcefield you often do not want to immediately replace the consumed Drone, because the economics shift.
insights.author: amalloy
insights.context: Discussion of defensive unit comparisons and economy implications.

insights.insight: Forcefields in base set are rarely worth buying because if Conduit costs 3 (making Green equal cost to Gold), you are paying 1 Gold + 1 Green = 2 resources to gain 1 HP, which at best saves one Drone. The efficiency is poor even at favorable cost ratios.
insights.author: velizar_
insights.context: Cost-benefit analysis of Forcefield purchases in base-set play.

insights.insight: Ebb (Ebbheart) is good not only in the late game when you have excess resources, but as soon as you stop buying Drones. Every turn you skip buying a Drone means a Drone is worth less than 3 gold at that moment, making Ebb's sell ability immediately valuable.
insights.author: jamberine, amalloy
insights.context: Economic analysis of Ebb's utility timing.

insights.insight: Ebb is purchased when blue tech is not otherwise productive in the set, and the primary alternative purchase is Steelsplitter. In this context, Ebb's ability to convert Drones into Engineer value is significantly more useful than merely blocking for soak.
insights.author: jamberine
insights.context: Discussion of Ebb as a blue tech outlet.

insights.insight: Sentinel is often the most efficient unit in 3-color games with good soak (Infusion Grid, Xeno, similar absorbers), justifying higher engineer counts to generate gold for Sentinels.
insights.author: masn6811
insights.context: Analysis of Sentinel efficiency in soak-heavy game contexts.

insights.insight: Spender supply (available absorbers and tech options in all three colors) indicates when lower engineer counts might be playable because you won't lack gold sinks.
insights.author: masn6811
insights.context: Discussion of how unit availability affects engineer count decisions.

insights.insight: Buying tech buildings for specific legendary units and then running out of that unit's supply forces inefficient spending of the tech; conversely, sometimes overtecking just to access a legendary unit is justified.
insights.author: awaclus
insights.context: Discussion of overextension and inefficient tech spending around legendary units.

insights.insight: Felid is strong even as a first attacker when Animus is available; the Bombarder rule (buy some permanent attack before Bombarder) suggests buying all available Felid supply when paired with Animus.
insights.author: velizar_
insights.context: Discussion of Felid's strength relative to other early attackers.

insights.insight: Evis requires Red mana to click and this cost is easy to forget during gameplay; proper costing analysis must account for the click cost in addition to purchase cost.
insights.author: velizar_
insights.context: Brief note on Evis mechanics to avoid missed opportunities.

insights.insight: Colossus can be purchased just for the blue sink (Clunk) value when you need to avoid floating blue, and buying Colossus for blue absorption is sometimes correct even in Splitter-focused games; this strategy can backfire if opponent shoots the Animus.
insights.author: masn6811
insights.context: Discussion of unusual Colossus applications for blue sink purposes.

insights.insight: Proto Doom effectively counters Asteri Venge by forcing the opponent's red tech into Proto absorb instead of allowing Venge to be aggressive, reducing Venge's value.
insights.author: silentslayers, spiritfryer
insights.context: Discussion of Doom, Venge, and Proto interactions in a complex set.

insights.insight: Xeno's best use is hard countering freeze effects, not just serving as a general absorber.
insights.author: pikachumemes.
insights.context: Clarification on Xeno's primary strength vs other absorption options.
